summary: Excellent performance with standout Dark Star and Help>Slip>Franklin's; recording sounds clear enough to capture crowd energy.
review: The show is remembered as a killer night, highlighted by a soaring Dark Star, a blistering Help / Slip / Franklin’s sequence, and powerful renditions of Stagger Lee, Death Don't Have No Mercy and Music Never Stopped. Reviewers note the band’s tight connection with the audience – especially Jerry’s groove – and there are no complaints about audio quality, suggesting a solid listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Single five‑star review praises both performance and sound; high show quality drives rating while recording quality is assumed good but not explicitly detailed.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific technical complaints; reviewers could hear crowd interaction clearly, implying a listenable source.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Review emphasizes strong momentum, with crowd‑driven energy especially around Dark Star and the transition into Drums/Space.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as grooving to the crowd; his playing is highlighted during Dark Star and Music Never Stopped.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned (as Bobby) wanting to jump into The Other One, indicating active involvement.
